vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: Adding a certmap section to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf follows standard STIG hardening for SSSD. The change is limited to authentication mapping and does not modify core system services or boot settings. While misconfiguration could affect login, the risk is low if the file is backed up and the config is tested before deployment.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; potential authentication failure if syntax is incorrect; possible loss of remote login if SSSD fails to start
